title: Introducing PulseProof
text: PulseProof is a trusted second screen for World Cup fans. It turns verified live match data into spoiler safe Catch Up, match communities and a non transferable fan identity on chain. The journey starts before kickoff, continues during play and remains useful after the final whistle.

title: TxLINE-powered Live Center
text: TxLINE is the primary live signal. PulseProof normalizes activated devnet fixtures and events into a public Server Sent Events stream. The Live Center keeps World Cup competition labels, official team abbreviations, flags, live status, finished results, upcoming fixtures, stadiums and kickoff times in one view with clear source labels.

title: Complete replay with Match brief and timeline
text: Finished matches use the same interface as live coverage. France versus England begins with the final score of four to six, then moves into Match brief and the complete TxLINE event timeline. The replay shows Jude Bellingham's goal at ninety plus eight, Trevoh Chalobah's substitution, created chances, attacking pressure and two VAR reviews, each with its source sequence number. Spoiler Shield hides results everywhere. Catch Up reveals only the selected event prefix. Signed relay and offline recap never expose future moments.

title: One wallet across the product
text: Phantom stays connected while the user moves from Live Center to Fan Zone. The profile belongs to the wallet, so the display name, points, streak and inventory follow without a second connection.

title: Wallet-owned display name
text: Profile editing creates a readable alias for match chat. The new name is prepared on the Fan Alias account on chain. It is not stored as a temporary browser preference.

title: Alias transaction and receipt
text: Phantom shows the real devnet transaction. After approval, the page updates immediately to Cryptovuive twenty six. It displays a confirmed receipt and Explorer link, then keeps the new alias after reload.

title: Daily on-chain check-in
text: Daily check in uses Solana time. The interface previews seven streak days and disables duplicate claims within the same UTC day.

title: Immediate check-in state
text: One Phantom approval creates the check in proof. The page immediately marks today complete, raises the streak and points, and shows a confirmed devnet receipt without any reload.

title: Ten thousand World Cup quiz variants
text: The daily quiz draws five questions from ten thousand deterministic World Cup variations built from sourced facts. Answer links stay hidden and explanations appear only after selection. This run scores five out of five. Daily rewards are single use, while practice rounds remain unlimited and provide no rewards.

title: Authorized quiz points
text: Phantom signs the authorized points claim. Seventy points arrive immediately, the button changes to Claimed today, and the transaction receipt remains independently verifiable on Explorer.

title: The complete reward catalog
text: The reward catalog contains thirty six original and non transferable medals, badges, avatar frames and characters. Limited editions always show a clear closing date. We scroll through every item before randomly selecting Bolt Keeper. Prices are fixed by the catalog on chain, so the client cannot invent a cheaper redemption.

title: Atomic redeem and equip
text: The transaction completes atomically: Bolt Keeper becomes owned and active, and the balance drops by one hundred sixty points.

title: Fixture-scoped match chat
text: Chat belongs to each match, beside the live or replay timeline. The composer uses the alias stored on chain and blocks links, betting, wallet secrets and anonymous impersonation.

title: Wallet-signed community message
text: Posting requires a fresh Phantom message signature, not a fee. After approval, the server verifies the wallet, alias, fixture and replay protection value. The signed message appears instantly as Cryptovuive twenty six in the England and Argentina room.

title: Eight fresh production checks
text: Finally, Judge Room runs the product instead of trusting slides. Eight fresh checks validate production identity, the World Cup catalog, public SSE, spoiler isolation, a signed Catch Up Capsule, Ed25519 verification inside the browser, the live Solana programs and the offline cache boundary. This run passes eight of eight and exposes current points, alias, finalized quiz and reward receipts, Explorer, CI and the public repository.

title: Every match leaves a memory
text: PulseProof turns live context into community identity and verifiable memory. Before, during and after the match, every match leaves a memory.
